companies.name: NVIDIA
companies.summary: NVIDIA's 2026 Annual Meeting of Stockholders is online in two days, June 24 at 9 a.m. PT — the first stockholder meeting since Vera Rubin entered full production and the Korea AI factory agreements were signed. Jensen Huang's Seoul visit (June 5–8) anchored South Korea as a gigawatt-scale AI factory market: SK Telecom (DSX-based AI cloud, first factory 2027), LG Group (robotics and autonomous-driving factory), SK hynix (next-gen HBM supply), NAVER (gigawatt-class sovereign AI infrastructure), and Hyundai Motor Group (autonomous driving). NVIDIA separately signed a partnership with data-center operator IREN carrying up to a $2.1 billion investment option, continuing its strategy of equity-backed infrastructure expansion. Vera Rubin remains in production at Anthropic, OpenAI, and SpaceXAI.

items.headline: NVIDIA Annual Meeting of Stockholders set for June 24 — two days away
items.detail: NVIDIA will hold its 2026 Annual Meeting of Stockholders online on June 24, 2026 at 9 a.m. PT. The meeting comes as NVIDIA's FY2026 AI semiconductor revenue remains on track for approximately $56 billion, Vera Rubin systems are in production at major AI labs, and the Korea AI factory partnership wave is the freshest strategic development. Non-stockholders may attend via guest login at the virtual meeting portal.

items.headline: Jensen Huang's Seoul visit produced gigawatt-scale AI factory agreements with SK Telecom, LG Group, SK hynix, NAVER, and Hyundai
items.detail: NVIDIA deepened South Korea alliances across a four-day visit by CEO Jensen Huang (June 5–8), securing agreements spanning gigawatt-scale AI cloud (SK Telecom using NVIDIA DSX, first factory 2027), AI factory for robotics and autonomous driving (LG Group), next-generation HBM supply (SK hynix), sovereign AI infrastructure scaled toward 1 GW (NAVER), and autonomous driving (Hyundai Motor Group). Jensen Huang told reporters a one-gigawatt AI factory costs roughly $60 billion, placing total South Korea investment potential at up to $360 billion over five years.

items.headline: NVIDIA signed data-center partnership with IREN carrying up to $2.1 billion investment option
items.detail: NVIDIA signed a partnership with data-center operator IREN that includes an option to invest up to $2.1 billion, extending its equity-anchored infrastructure expansion strategy alongside its existing CoreWeave (~$2 billion) stake. The IREN agreement adds another GPU-cloud operator to NVIDIA's network of infrastructure partners building Vera Rubin-based AI factories.

companies.name: Alphabet / Google DeepMind
companies.category: Foundation models, search, cloud, and AI research
companies.summary: Gemini 3.5 Flash remains the default model in the Gemini consumer app, AI Mode in Search, and Vertex/Gemini API at $1.50/$9 per million input/output tokens. Gemini 3.5 Pro — committed for a June release by CEO Sundar Pichai at Google I/O — is still in limited preview for select Vertex AI enterprise customers as of June 19, with eight days remaining on the self-imposed June 30 window. Pro targets a 2-million-token context window, a Deep Think reasoning mode, and frontier multimodal understanding. Apple's reported $1 billion per year Gemini deal powering a rebuilt Siri AI remains Google DeepMind's largest known enterprise model distribution contract, with a September 2026 launch on Google Cloud NVIDIA Blackwell infrastructure.

items.headline: Gemini 3.5 Pro in limited enterprise preview with 8 days to June 30 self-imposed deadline
items.detail: Gemini 3.5 Pro remains in limited preview for select Vertex AI enterprise customers only as of June 19, 2026. CEO Sundar Pichai committed to a June release window at Google I/O on May 20; with eight days remaining on the deadline, the model has not shipped to the public Gemini app, Google AI Studio, or consumer subscriptions. Pro targets a 2-million-token context window — double Flash — a Deep Think reasoning mode for hard multi-step problems, and frontier multimodal understanding. Expected pricing is roughly 10x Flash: approximately $15 per million input tokens and $60 per million output tokens.

items.headline: Gemini 3.5 Flash is the default Gemini model across consumer app, Search, and API at $1.50/$9 per million tokens
items.detail: Gemini 3.5 Flash — announced at Google I/O 2026 on May 20 — is the default Gemini model in the consumer app, AI Mode in Google Search, and Vertex AI / Gemini API. The model delivers 289 tokens per second (4x faster than prior frontier models), outperforms Gemini 3.1 Pro on coding and agentic benchmarks, and is priced at $1.50 per million input tokens and $9 per million output tokens — meaningfully cheaper than the Gemini 3 tier it replaces.

items.headline: Apple's reported $1B/year Gemini deal to power Siri AI launching September 2026 on Google Cloud NVIDIA infrastructure
items.detail: Apple's reported $1 billion per year agreement for Google Gemini to power a rebuilt Siri AI — announced at WWDC 2026 (June 8) — remains Google DeepMind's largest known enterprise model distribution contract. Siri AI is expected to launch September 2026 on Google Cloud infrastructure powered by NVIDIA Blackwell chips, reaching an estimated 2.2 billion active Apple devices. The EU launch is delayed pending unresolved Digital Markets Act regulatory talks.

companies.name: Microsoft
companies.category: Cloud AI, copilots, and enterprise software
companies.summary: Microsoft Copilot Cowork usage-based billing has been active since June 19; frontline tenants have a grace period to July 1 — nine days away. Microsoft 365 Business Standard and Business Premium with Copilot become permanent commercial SKUs July 1 alongside a global price update. Microsoft IQ (Work IQ + Web IQ) is GA across GitHub Copilot, Foundry, and Copilot Studio. Microsoft is exploring hosting a fine-tuned DeepSeek or comparable open-source model as a lower-cost foundation for some Copilot Cowork workloads. New Agentic AI certifications AB-100 and AB-620 are entering the catalog as legacy Copilot Applied Skills retire June 30.

items.headline: Microsoft Copilot Cowork usage-based billing active since June 19; July 1 grace period deadline for frontline tenants
items.detail: Microsoft Copilot Cowork has transitioned to usage-based pricing with billing active as of June 19; frontline preview tenants have until July 1 — nine days — to complete transition. Microsoft 365 Business Standard with Copilot and Business Premium with Copilot also formalize as permanent commercial SKUs on July 1, with a global price update accompanying the change.

items.headline: Microsoft IQ GA across GitHub Copilot, Foundry, and Copilot Studio; AB-100 and AB-620 certifications launching
items.detail: Microsoft IQ — combining Work IQ APIs (organizational context inside the M365 trust boundary) and Web IQ (real-time web grounding) — is generally available across GitHub Copilot, Microsoft Foundry, and Copilot Studio as of June 16. New agent-focused certifications Agentic AI Business Solutions Architect (AB-100) and AI Agent Builder Associate (AB-620) are entering the catalog as some legacy Copilot Applied Skills certifications retire June 30.

items.headline: Microsoft exploring fine-tuned DeepSeek or open-source model to reduce Copilot Cowork API costs
items.detail: Axios reported Microsoft is evaluating hosting a fine-tuned version of DeepSeek or a comparable open-source model as a lower-cost foundation for some Copilot Cowork workloads — reducing dependency on Anthropic and OpenAI API costs as agentic token consumption scales into enterprise deployments. The exploration does not affect Claude's position in Microsoft Foundry or GPT-5.5's status as the primary Copilot foundation model.

companies.name: OpenAI
companies.summary: The GPT-5.6 prediction market window opened today (June 22), though OpenAI has made no official announcement; prediction markets now give 42% probability to a June 22–28 launch and 58% to no release by June 28, a reversal from yesterday's 71%/29% split. Noam Shazeer — co-inventor of the Transformer attention mechanism — joined OpenAI on June 18 as Lead for Architecture Research, its highest-profile researcher hire in years. GPT-5.6's internal release candidate (codename 'kindle-alpha') briefly appeared on the Design Arena testing platform before being pulled. OpenAI's confidential S-1 was filed June 8; CFO Sarah Friar has reportedly expressed concern about 2026 readiness with a potential delay to 2027. ChatGPT's global AI assistant market share fell to 46.4% in late May — the first time below 50% — with Gemini at 27.7% and Claude at 10.3%.

items.headline: GPT-5.6 launch window opens today; prediction markets now 42% for June 22–28 release; Noam Shazeer joins as Lead for Architecture Research
items.detail: June 22 opens the primary prediction window for GPT-5.6 but OpenAI has made no official announcement. Polymarket odds shifted to 42% for a June 22–28 launch and 58% for no release by June 28. GPT-5.6 — described by chief scientist Jakub Pachocki as a 'meaningful improvement' — features a redesigned reward audit pipeline to address alignment failures in GPT-5.5's training data, an expected 1.5 million token context window (43% larger than GPT-5.5), and roughly 10–15% token efficiency gains. Separately, Noam Shazeer — co-inventor of the Transformer attention mechanism and former Google Brain researcher — joined OpenAI on June 18 as Lead for Architecture Research, its highest-profile researcher hire since the GPT-4 era.

items.headline: OpenAI S-1 filed June 8; CFO may push to 2027; ChatGPT market share fell below 50% for first time
items.detail: OpenAI confirmed its confidential S-1 filing with the SEC on June 8, 2026, with Goldman Sachs, Morgan Stanley, and JPMorgan leading. CFO Sarah Friar has reportedly expressed concern about operational and compliance readiness, with a potential delay to 2027. Sensor Tower's 2026 State of AI Report found ChatGPT's share of the global AI assistant market fell to 46.4% by late May — the first time below 50% — with Gemini at 27.7% and Claude at 10.3%. EU AI Act chatbot enforcement begins August 2, 2026, adding compliance milestones to the pre-IPO period.

items.headline: Amazon dropped a nearly finished Hollywood film about Sam Altman citing the $50 billion OpenAI deal
items.detail: Amazon dropped a nearly finished Hollywood film about OpenAI CEO Sam Altman, reportedly due to the $50 billion OpenAI-Amazon cloud deal creating a commercial conflict of interest. The development illustrates how OpenAI's expanding corporate partnerships are reaching into unexpected areas of Amazon's business.

companies.name: Anthropic
companies.summary: Day 10 of the Fable 5 and Mythos 5 global suspension. The Fable 5 free-trial window closed today (June 22); the credit-based pricing model — $10/$50 per million input/output tokens — begins tomorrow (June 23). Fable 5 resurfaced in the Claude Android app's coding interface on June 21 with a changed 'rate-limiting' error rather than the prior hard-offline message, still the strongest early-restoration signal. Anthropic's Managing Director of International Chris Ciauri said on June 17 he was 'very confident' the models would return 'in coming days'; that date has passed. The White House June 2 Executive Order created a 30-day pre-release review framework. Trump told Axios (June 20) he no longer views Anthropic as a national security threat. Prediction markets give 57% odds of US restoration before July 1. Anthropic's July 8 privacy policy update creates a biometric ID verification path for US citizens. Revenue run rate has surpassed $30 billion.

items.headline: Fable 5 free-trial window closed today (June 22); credit model at $10/$50 per million tokens starts tomorrow
items.detail: The Fable 5 and Mythos 5 free-trial window for paid subscribers — Pro, Max, Team, and Enterprise — officially closed today, June 22. The credit-based pricing model at $10 per million input tokens and $50 per million output tokens begins June 23. Anthropic has not issued updated pricing guidance for how the suspended models will be billed once access is restored. The trial closure with models still offline represents a material customer impact entering day 10 of the suspension.

items.headline: Fable 5 resurfaces in Claude Android app; NSA Director testimony cites Mythos breaching classified systems; 57% odds of US restoration by July 1
items.detail: On June 21, developers reported Fable 5 reappearing in the Claude Android app's coding interface model selector — invocation attempts returned 'server is temporarily rate-limiting requests' rather than the prior hard-offline message, the strongest early-restoration signal yet. The Economist reported the NSA Director testified Mythos 5 breached nearly all classified systems within hours of testing, now the most-cited official justification for the ban. Polymarket assigns 57% odds of Fable 5 US restoration before July 1 and 75% by July 17.

items.headline: Anthropic's July 8 privacy policy enables biometric ID verification as potential Fable access path; Globe and Mail reports active deal-making with Trump administration
items.detail: Anthropic's updated privacy policy effective July 8, 2026 expands collection of government-issued ID documents, facial images, and biometric facial geometry templates via Persona — creating a compliant mechanism to restore Fable 5 for verified US citizens without requiring the full Commerce Department ban to be lifted. The Globe and Mail reported active deal-making between Anthropic and Trump administration officials toward a joint AI risk framework. Trump's post-G7 statement (June 20) that Anthropic is no longer a national security threat remains the clearest political signal, though the Commerce Department export control directive has not been formally withdrawn.

companies.name: Meta
companies.category: Open models, consumer AI, and infrastructure
companies.summary: Meta Llama 4 continues to be actively evaluated as the leading enterprise self-hostable alternative during the Fable 5 ban period. Meta is testing a 'Meta One' subscription umbrella unifying Facebook Plus, Instagram Plus, WhatsApp Plus, and AI product subscriptions. Meta AI Mode on Facebook (launched June 15) synthesizes answers from public posts across Groups and Reels for Meta's 3.2 billion daily active users. Meta's 2026 capex guidance of $115–135 billion is unchanged. AIPCon 10 revealed Palantir Q1 revenue grew 85% as enterprise AI deployment accelerates.

items.headline: Meta Llama 4 positioned as leading enterprise self-hostable alternative during Fable 5 ban
items.detail: As the Fable 5 and Mythos 5 global suspension enters day 10, Meta Llama 4 has emerged as the primary self-hostable frontier-class model drawing enterprise evaluation. Enterprises seeking to reduce exposure to government model access revocation risk are evaluating Llama 4's open-weights posture as a hedge. Mistral AI's CEO Arthur Mensch has made similar sovereignty arguments; both reflect the industry's acute awareness of model access risk as a procurement variable following the June 12 suspension.
items.sourceTitle: Build Fast With AI / The New Stack

items.headline: Meta testing 'Meta One' subscription umbrella to unify Facebook, Instagram, WhatsApp, and AI product subscriptions
items.detail: Meta is testing a 'Meta One' subscription brand designed to unify Facebook Plus, Instagram Plus, WhatsApp Plus, and forthcoming AI product subscriptions into a single membership tier. The move is part of Meta's push to build sustainable subscription revenue alongside advertising as 2026 capex of $115–135 billion scales.

items.headline: Meta AI Mode on Facebook synthesizes answers from public posts across Groups and Reels for 3.2B DAUs
items.detail: Meta AI Mode on Facebook — launched June 15 — lets users ask plain-language questions and receive synthesized answers drawn from public posts, Groups, and Reels across the platform. The feature uses Meta AI to surface community-sourced knowledge for Meta's 3.2 billion daily active users, positioning Facebook as an AI-powered information layer alongside its traditional social feed.

companies.name: Amazon / AWS
companies.summary: AWS Summit New York (June 17–20) concluded with four flagship enterprise releases: Amazon Bedrock AgentCore GA (Managed Knowledge Base, Web Search, Agent Optimization, A/B testing, intent analytics, Guardrails), AWS Continuum (AI-native security), AWS Context (knowledge graph for agents), and EC2 G7 instances with NVIDIA RTX PRO 4500 Blackwell Server Edition GPUs (4.6x AI inference). Amazon Bedrock now serves as the enterprise multi-model control plane hosting Anthropic Claude, OpenAI GPT-5.5, and xAI Grok 4.3 under unified AWS governance. Southwest Airlines named AWS as preferred cloud partner for AI-enabled architecture by 2028.

items.headline: AWS Summit New York concluded June 20 with AgentCore GA, AWS Continuum, AWS Context, and EC2 G7 as flagship enterprise releases
items.detail: AWS Summit New York (June 17–20) produced four flagship enterprise releases: Amazon Bedrock AgentCore GA (with Managed Knowledge Base, Web Search, Agent Optimization, A/B testing, intent analytics, and Guardrails for prompt injection), AWS Continuum (AI-native security for agentic workloads), AWS Context (knowledge graph for agents), and EC2 G7 instances with NVIDIA RTX PRO 4500 Blackwell Server Edition GPUs delivering 4.6x AI inference performance — making AWS the first major cloud to support this GPU class.

items.headline: Amazon Bedrock is the enterprise multi-model control plane hosting Claude, GPT-5.5, and Grok 4.3 under unified governance
items.detail: Following AgentCore GA, Grok 4.3 on Bedrock (June 15), and OpenAI frontier models and Codex (June 1), Amazon Bedrock serves as the enterprise control plane where companies access Anthropic Claude, OpenAI GPT-5.5, and xAI Grok under unified AWS governance, compliance, billing, and security. AgentCore provides the runtime for multi-model agentic workflows, positioning Bedrock as the enterprise multi-model distribution hub.

items.headline: Southwest Airlines named AWS as preferred cloud partner for AI-enabled architecture by 2028
items.detail: Southwest Airlines announced AWS as its preferred cloud provider to modernize its technology foundation and apply AI, with plans to transition to a cloud-based, AI- and agent-enabled architecture on AWS by 2028. The airline partnership reflects AWS's continued expansion beyond hyperscale tech into regulated, logistics-intensive enterprise verticals.

companies.name: xAI
companies.summary: SpaceX (SPCX) continues to trade around $210–220, up ~56% from its $135 June 12 IPO price. The $60 billion all-stock Anysphere (Cursor AI) acquisition confirmed via SEC Form 8-K is expected to close Q3 2026; Cursor had over 1 million paying users, more than half the Fortune 500 as clients, and more than $2 billion ARR. Grok for Microsoft Word (free add-in, launched June 18) embeds live web research and diagram generation in Word, with PowerPoint and Excel versions also available. Grok 4.3 is GA on Amazon Bedrock with a 1-million-token context window.

items.headline: SPCX trading ~$210–220, up ~56% from IPO; $60B Cursor acquisition expected Q3 close
items.detail: SpaceX (SPCX) continues trading around $210–220, up approximately 56% from its June 12 IPO price of $135 following the largest IPO ever recorded ($75 billion raised at a $1.75 trillion valuation). SpaceX confirmed via SEC Form 8-K the $60 billion all-stock acquisition of Anysphere (Cursor AI), expected to receive regulatory clearance and close Q3 2026. Cursor had over 1 million paying users, more than half of Fortune 500 companies as clients, and more than $2 billion ARR — the fastest B2B software company to reach that milestone.

items.headline: xAI launched Grok for Microsoft Word as a free Microsoft 365 add-in with live research and diagram generation
items.detail: xAI released Grok for Microsoft Word — a free Microsoft 365 add-in launched June 18 — that embeds Grok's conversational AI, real-time web search (via xAI's engine plus Brave and Bing), and Mermaid-based diagram generation directly into Word's side panel. PowerPoint and Excel versions are also available. xAI confirmed no premium tiers, no token limits on document interactions, and no plans to charge during 2026, positioning Grok for Word as a direct zero-cost alternative to Microsoft's $30/user/month Copilot.

items.headline: Grok 4.3 GA on Amazon Bedrock; Grok powers Gopuff's AI shopping assistant and Databricks Agent Bricks
items.detail: Grok 4.3 is generally available on Amazon Bedrock with a 1-million-token context window, the lowest reported hallucination rate among frontier models, and configurable reasoning effort. Grok also serves as the AI engine inside Gopuff's 'Go' shopping assistant and is available natively on Databricks Agent Bricks (announced at Data + AI Summit, June 18), giving enterprise data teams access to Grok for agentic data workflows inside the Databricks lakehouse platform.

companies.name: Broadcom
companies.category: AI networking, custom silicon, and infrastructure software
companies.summary: Broadcom (AVGO) went ex-dividend today, June 22, with a $0.65 quarterly cash dividend payable June 30 — marking 14 consecutive years of dividend increases. Q2 FY2026 AI semiconductor revenue was $10.8 billion (+143% YoY) on record quarterly revenue and free cash flow; Q3 guidance is approximately $16 billion; FY2026 target is approximately $56 billion; FY2027 target exceeds $100 billion across six core custom-silicon customers including Anthropic, Google, and Meta. Broadcom's Tomahawk 6 (Davisson) — the industry's first 102.4 Tbps Ethernet switch with co-packaged optics — remains the most significant new product.

items.headline: Broadcom ex-dividend today (June 22); $0.65/share payable June 30; Q2 AI revenue $10.8B up 143% YoY; Q3 guidance ~$16B
items.detail: Broadcom (AVGO) passed its ex-dividend date today, June 22, 2026, with a $0.65 per share quarterly cash dividend payable June 30. Q2 FY2026 AI semiconductor revenue hit $10.8 billion on record quarterly revenue, operating profit, and free cash flow. Q3 AI semiconductor revenue guidance is approximately $16 billion. Broadcom's FY2026 AI semiconductor revenue target remains approximately $56 billion, and the FY2027 target exceeds $100 billion driven by six core custom-silicon customers. The dividend marks 14 consecutive years of increases.

items.headline: Broadcom Tomahawk 6 (Davisson): industry's first 102.4 Tbps Ethernet switch with co-packaged optics
items.detail: The Tomahawk 6 (codename Davisson), unveiled in mid-June 2026, is the industry's first 102.4 Tbps Ethernet switch silicon with co-packaged optics, addressing bandwidth demands of AI data centers where conventional pluggable optics are insufficient at hyperscale. JPMorgan maintains an 'aggressive buy' rating on AVGO; 48 analysts hold a 'Strong Buy' consensus with a $522 12-month price target.

items.headline: Broadcom's reiterated Q3 guidance triggered 10% AMD sell-off and broader AI chip sector contagion on June 9
items.detail: When Broadcom reiterated rather than raised its Q3 AI revenue guidance on June 3, Wall Street's expectations of a guidance upgrade were unmet, triggering a broad AI chip sell-off on June 9 that dragged AMD down approximately 10%. The sell-off reflected market expectations of accelerating guidance; Broadcom's underlying fundamentals — $10.8B Q2 AI revenue, $56B FY2026 target, six custom-silicon customers — remain strong.

companies.name: Palantir
companies.summary: Palantir Q1 2026 revenue jumped 85% year-over-year to $1.6 billion while US commercial growth hit 133%, driving a 10-point full-year guidance raise. Maven Smart System usage quadrupled over 12 months and retains DoD program-of-record status. AIPCon 10 (June 4) showcased AI amplifying tradecraft at Kirkland & Ellis, McCarthy Building, USDA, Hertz, and others. Palantir AIP is now available on Google Cloud Marketplace with BigQuery and Gemini integrations. The Anthropic-Palantir-AWS defense pipeline remains affected by the Fable/Mythos suspension; Trump's June 20 Axios statement has not yet produced a formal Commerce Department reversal.

items.headline: Palantir Q1 revenue +85% YoY to $1.6B; US commercial +133%; Maven usage quadrupled over 12 months
items.detail: Palantir reported Q1 2026 revenue of $1.6 billion, up 85% year-over-year, with US commercial revenue growing 133% — the fastest commercial growth in company history. Maven Smart System usage quadrupled over the prior 12 months. Palantir raised its full-year guidance by 10 points following the results, reflecting AIP's accelerating enterprise and government deployment momentum.

items.headline: Palantir AIPCon 10 showcased AI amplifying customer tradecraft; AIP available on Google Cloud Marketplace
items.detail: Palantir hosted AIPCon 10 on June 4, 2026, featuring customer-led demonstrations from Kirkland & Ellis, McCarthy Building, USDA, Hertz, Nscale, Accenture, and Parts Town. Palantir AIP is now available on Google Cloud Marketplace with BigQuery data access and Gemini model integrations, expanding cloud distribution beyond AWS and Azure.

items.headline: Maven Smart System retains DoD program-of-record status; Anthropic-Palantir-AWS defense pipeline under strain
items.detail: Maven Smart System retains its DoD program-of-record designation with multi-year protected funding across five military branches. Trump's June 20 Axios statement that Anthropic is no longer a national security threat has not yet produced a formal Commerce Department reversal or restoration of Pentagon-Anthropic supply chain access. Claude remains integrated in Maven for eligible workflows pending resolution of the export control directive.

companies.name: Apple
companies.summary: Apple is reportedly in early-stage internal discussions about acquiring Perplexity AI at approximately $14 billion — which would be Apple's largest acquisition ever. The $1 billion per year Google Gemini deal powering a rebuilt Siri AI remains the defining Apple AI story with a September 2026 launch on Google Cloud NVIDIA Blackwell infrastructure. The iOS 27 Extensions framework (allowing ChatGPT, Claude, or Gemini inside Siri) remains disabled. Bloomberg reports OpenAI is weighing a breach-of-contract notice over the perceived lack of prime ChatGPT placement. Apple also reportedly considered acquiring Mistral AI.

items.headline: Apple reportedly in early internal discussions about acquiring Perplexity AI at ~$14B — would be Apple's largest acquisition ever
items.detail: Bloomberg reports Apple executives including Adrian Perica and Eddy Cue have held early internal discussions about potentially acquiring Perplexity AI at approximately $14 billion — which would be Apple's largest acquisition ever, dwarfing the $3 billion Beats deal in 2014. The discussions are described as early-stage and 'may not lead to an offer.' Perplexity has stated it is unaware of ongoing or prospective M&A talks. CEO Aravind Srinivas has publicly stated a preference for an independent 2028 IPO.

items.headline: Apple's reported $1B/year Gemini deal powers rebuilt Siri AI launching September 2026; iOS 27 Extensions disabled
items.detail: Apple's reported $1 billion per year deal for Google Gemini to power a rebuilt Siri AI — announced at WWDC 2026 (June 8) — remains active with a September 2026 launch target on Google Cloud NVIDIA Blackwell infrastructure. The iOS 27 developer beta contains a working Extensions framework that would allow users to choose ChatGPT, Claude, or Gemini inside Siri, but Apple has disabled it pending DMA regulatory clarity and resolution with OpenAI, which Bloomberg reports is weighing a breach-of-contract notice.

items.headline: Apple also considered acquiring Mistral AI alongside Perplexity, per Sherwood News
items.detail: Sherwood News reported that Apple considered acquiring Mistral AI alongside Perplexity AI as part of Apple's exploration of AI strategy options beyond its existing OpenAI partnership. CEO Arthur Mensch has not confirmed acquisition discussions. The reports signal Apple is actively evaluating multiple AI acquisition paths as it builds out its AI strategy beyond the Gemini-for-Siri partnership.

companies.name: AMD
companies.category: AI accelerators, CPUs, and data center silicon
companies.summary: AMD CEO Lisa Su confirmed MI450 GPU samples are shipping to lead customers including OpenAI and Meta. AMD denied MI455X delay reports, stating Helios rack systems remain on target for H2 2026. The MEXT acquisition (completed June 15) brings predictive memory technology making flash storage behave like DRAM for AI workloads. Rackspace signed a 30 MW AMD AI Compute deployment agreement June 16. AMD Advancing AI 2026 is a two-day event on July 22–23 in San Francisco — the next major disclosure milestone. AMD reports Q2 2026 results August 4.

items.headline: AMD MI450 GPU samples shipping to OpenAI and Meta; Helios on target for H2 2026 despite delay reports
items.detail: AMD CEO Lisa Su confirmed MI450 GPUs are sampling to lead customers including OpenAI and Meta. AMD denied reports of MI455X delays, stating Helios rack systems — AMD's NVIDIA Vera Rubin competitor — remain on target for H2 2026 production. MI450 delivers up to 40 PFLOPs FP4 performance. Early customer engagement on the successor MI500 architecture is also active.

items.headline: AMD completed MEXT acquisition June 15; signed Rackspace 30 MW AMD AI Compute deployment June 16
items.detail: AMD completed the acquisition of MEXT on June 15, bringing predictive memory technology that makes flash storage behave like DRAM for AI workloads — a critical bottleneck for large-scale inference. On June 16, AMD signed a definitive agreement with Rackspace Technology for a phased 30 MW AMD AI Compute deployment, adding cloud capacity for MI450-class workloads.

items.headline: AMD Advancing AI 2026 is a two-day event July 22–23 in San Francisco — next major milestone for MI450, OpenAI, and Meta deployment disclosures
items.detail: AMD's Advancing AI 2026 event is a two-day in-person and livestreamed event on July 22–23 at Moscone Center in San Francisco — the next major disclosure milestone. AMD chair and CEO Lisa Su will join ecosystem partners, customers, and developers to share end-to-end AI solutions. Expected disclosures include formal MI450 series launch, EPYC Venice Zen 6 CPUs, Helios rack confirmation, and a preview of the MI500 series targeting 2027. AMD reports Q2 2026 financial results on August 4.

companies.name: Perplexity AI
companies.summary: Apple is reportedly in early internal discussions about a ~$14 billion acquisition of Perplexity AI — which would be Apple's largest deal ever. CEO Aravind Srinivas has not confirmed acquisition discussions and maintains the company's stated preference for a 2028 IPO. Perplexity's $500M ARR (April 2026, 335% YoY growth), 100M+ MAU, and $20B valuation from the May 2026 round represent the current price anchors. Perplexity Computer is live as native side panels in Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Outlook, and Teams; the Comet AI browser reached #3 in the App Store within 48 hours of its iOS launch.

items.headline: Apple reportedly considering ~$14B Perplexity AI acquisition in early-stage internal discussions
items.detail: Bloomberg reports Apple executives Adrian Perica and Eddy Cue held early internal discussions about potentially acquiring Perplexity AI at approximately $14 billion. The discussions are described as early-stage and 'may not lead to an offer.' Perplexity confirmed it is unaware of ongoing or prospective M&A talks. Apple CFO Kevan Parekh mentioned Perplexity's Personal Computer product at Q2 2026 earnings. CEO Srinivas has publicly stated a preference for a 2028 IPO. Perplexity's $20B May 2026 round valuation sets the acquisition premium floor.

items.headline: CEO Srinivas confirmed 2028 IPO target; $500M ARR, 100M+ MAU, $20B valuation from May 2026 round
items.detail: CEO Aravind Srinivas confirmed the 2028 IPO target on June 9, 2026 as a deliberate strategy regardless of Anthropic and OpenAI moving toward public markets. ARR reached $500 million in April 2026 (335% year-over-year growth), with 100M+ monthly active users and a $20B valuation from the May 2026 fundraising round. The 2028 target reflects a strategy to reach maximum scale before public markets.

items.headline: Perplexity Computer live as native Microsoft 365 side panels; Comet browser reached #3 App Store on iOS launch
items.detail: Perplexity's Computer agentic tool is live as native side panels in Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Outlook, and Teams — directly competing with Microsoft Copilot inside Microsoft 365. The Comet AI browser is globally available and reached the #3 App Store position within 48 hours of its iOS launch, positioning Perplexity as a workplace AI layer and browser-level intelligence platform.

companies.name: CoreWeave
companies.summary: CoreWeave (CRWV) joined the Nasdaq-100 before market open today, June 22 — just 15 months post-IPO — one of the fastest index inclusions in recent tech history. Other June 22 additions: Astera Labs, Nebius Group, Rocket Lab, and Teradyne. CRWV was trading around $117–120 ahead of inclusion, up 18.87% over the prior week. Russell 3000 inclusion follows June 27. Contracted revenue backlog is approaching approximately $131 billion (up ~$32B since end-Q1). The 2026 capex floor was raised to $31 billion for Vera Rubin NVL72 buildout. A new $2.2 billion Chicago-area data-center lease adds contracted revenue visibility. Cerebras Q1 2026 earnings are tomorrow evening (June 23).

items.headline: CoreWeave joined Nasdaq-100 today (June 22) — one of the fastest index inclusions in recent tech history; Russell 3000 June 27
items.detail: CoreWeave (CRWV) officially joined the Nasdaq-100 Index before market open today, June 22, 2026 — just 15 months after its IPO at $40 per share, among the fastest index inclusions in recent tech sector history. Other additions: Astera Labs, Nebius Group, Rocket Lab, and Teradyne. CRWV traded around $117–120 ahead of inclusion, up 18.87% over the prior week as traders front-ran mandatory buying from QQQ and Nasdaq-100 tracking funds. Russell 3000 inclusion follows June 27.

items.headline: CoreWeave backlog approaching $131B; 2026 capex floor raised to $31B; $2.2B Chicago data-center lease adds visibility
items.detail: Analyst estimates put CoreWeave's contracted revenue backlog at approximately $131 billion, up roughly $32 billion since reporting $99.4 billion at end-Q1 2026. The 2026 capex floor was raised to $31 billion to accommodate Vera Rubin NVL72 commitments from Meta, Anthropic, and OpenAI. A new $2.2 billion Chicago-area data-center lease was announced ahead of Nasdaq-100 inclusion, directly attacking the bear case on CoreWeave's spending by adding contracted revenue and visibility.

items.headline: CoreWeave Q1 2026 revenue $2.1B (+112% YoY); Vera Rubin NVL72 bring-up completed June 1; Cerebras Q1 earnings tomorrow (June 23)
items.detail: CoreWeave reported Q1 2026 revenue of $2.1 billion (+112% YoY) in its first public earnings report and completed the industry-first Vera Rubin NVL72 system bring-up on June 1. The next major AI infrastructure earnings catalyst is Cerebras Systems' Q1 2026 release after market close tomorrow, June 23 — Cerebras' first quarterly report as a public company, with analysts estimating revenue of approximately $56.65M.

companies.name: Mistral AI
companies.summary: Mistral AI confirmed a $3.5 billion raise at a €20 billion valuation. Its Paris-area data center at Bruyères-le-Châtel is expected to open by end of June 2026 with 13,800–18,000 NVIDIA GB300 GPU systems. The NVIDIA-Mistral Mistral Compute sovereign AI GPU cloud for European customers was launched at VivaTech June 20. Mistral launched Vibe — a unified AI agent for work and code with Work Mode and Code Mode. Mistral acquired Emmi AI (Austrian industrial AI simulation). CEO Arthur Mensch continues to leverage European concern over the Fable ban to position Mistral as the open-source sovereignty alternative. Mistral targets €1 billion in 2026 revenue with 1,000 employees.

items.headline: Mistral confirmed $3.5B raise at €20B valuation; Paris data center opening end of June with 13,800+ NVIDIA GPU systems
items.detail: Mistral AI confirmed a $3.5 billion fundraise at a €20 billion valuation — nearly doubling its prior €11.7 billion September 2025 Series C. The Bruyères-le-Châtel data center near Paris is expected to open by end of June 2026 with 13,800 NVIDIA GB300 GPU systems, forming the first node in a planned 200 MW European compute roadmap. A secondary Les Ulis (Essonne) 10 MW inference facility is scheduled for Q3 2026. CEO Arthur Mensch confirmed 1,000 employees and a €1 billion 2026 revenue target.

items.headline: NVIDIA and Mistral's Mistral Compute sovereign AI GPU cloud launched at VivaTech; Vibe agent and Emmi AI acquisition announced
items.detail: NVIDIA and Mistral AI launched Mistral Compute — a sovereign AI GPU cloud platform for European customers and governments — at VivaTech 2026 (closing June 20). Jensen Huang highlighted Mistral Compute as Europe's AI sovereignty infrastructure example. Mistral also launched Vibe, a unified AI agent for work and code combining Work Mode (long-running tasks) and Code Mode (remote coding and pull requests) with a VS Code extension and CLI. Mistral acquired Austrian company Emmi AI, which develops AI simulation models for industrial engineering.

items.headline: CEO Mensch positions Mistral as open-source sovereignty alternative to US-controlled models amid Fable ban
items.detail: CEO Arthur Mensch is actively leveraging European concern over the Anthropic Fable/Mythos export suspension to position Mistral as the open-source alternative that governments cannot revoke. Mensch stated: 'We exist to make sure that everyone gets access to the best AI systems, outside of centralised control exercised by states or corporations.' Enterprise teams are actively evaluating Llama 4 and Mistral as sovereign alternatives during the ban.

companies.name: Tesla
companies.category: Autonomous vehicles, robotics, and applied AI
companies.summary: Cybercab production at Giga Texas continues to ramp with 102+ units in the outbound lot; the EPA filing (June 19) confirmed full vehicle specs (47.6 kWh battery, 163 kW motor, 418-mile unadjusted range). Both a Tesla Cybercab and a Cybertruck have been spotted fitted with robotaxi validation hardware, suggesting Tesla is broadening autonomous ride-hailing validation beyond the Cybercab alone. The robotaxi service covers Austin (full metro), Dallas, Houston, and the SF Bay Area. Optimus Gen 3 is in mass production at Fremont; the dedicated Optimus factory at Giga Texas North Campus targets 10 million robots per year. FSD v14.3 reduces inference latency 20%.

items.headline: Cybercab production ramp: 102+ units in Giga Texas outbound lot; EPA confirms key specs; Cybertruck also spotted with robotaxi validation hardware
items.detail: As of June 19, 2026, 102 Cybercab units were in the outbound lot at Giga Texas. The EPA filing confirmed full vehicle specifications: 47.6 kWh battery, 163 kW motor, 418-mile unadjusted range (293-mile adjusted), and 3,113 lb curb weight. Both a Cybercab and a Cybertruck have now been spotted fitted with specialized robotaxi validation hardware on their roofs, indicating Tesla is broadening autonomous ride-hailing validation to multiple vehicle platforms. Cybercabs are expected to enter active fleet service as early as July 2026.

items.headline: Optimus Gen 3 in mass production at Fremont; dedicated Optimus factory at Giga Texas North Campus targeting 10M robots/year
items.detail: Tesla's Optimus Gen 3 humanoid robot entered mass production at Fremont in January 2026, with V3 full-body production targeting summer 2026. Tesla's Q1 2026 report confirmed a 5.2 million square foot Optimus factory expansion at Gigafactory Texas North Campus targeting 10 million robots per year, with Fremont's Model S and X lines converted to a 1 million unit pilot line. Public Optimus sales target end of 2027 at $20,000–$30,000.

items.headline: FSD v14.3 cuts inference latency 20%; robotaxi service active in Austin, Dallas, Houston, SF Bay Area
items.detail: FSD (Supervised) v14.3 launched in April 2026 with a rewritten AI compiler reducing inference latency by up to 20% and an enhanced neural network vision encoder for low-visibility scenarios. The Cybercab robotaxi service covers the full Austin metro area, Dallas, Houston, and the SF Bay Area. Early users and the insurance industry have reported some long wait times and stalled rides, which Tesla has not formally addressed.

ipoTracker.status: Confirmed filing; Fable/Mythos ban day 10; free trial window closed today (June 22); credit model starts tomorrow ($10/$50 per million); 57% odds of US restoration by July 1
ipoTracker.process: Anthropic confirmed its confidential draft Form S-1 submission to the SEC on June 1, 2026, after closing a $65 billion Series H at a ~$965 billion post-money valuation (May 28). An October 2026 listing window is widely reported. As of June 22, Fable 5 and Mythos 5 remain offline globally (day 10). The Fable 5 free-trial window closed today; the credit-based model at $10/$50 per million input/output tokens starts June 23. Fable 5 resurfaced in the Claude Android app's coding interface on June 21 with a changed 'rate-limiting' error — the strongest early-restoration signal. Trump told Axios (June 20) he no longer views Anthropic as a national security threat, but no formal Commerce Department reversal has been issued. Anthropic's July 8 privacy policy update creates a biometric ID verification path for US citizen access. Polymarket gives 57% odds of US restoration before July 1. Revenue run rate has surpassed $30 billion.
ipoTracker.note: The June 22 free-trial window closure and June 23 credit model start are the key transitions today. Watch July 8 (biometric ID policy effective) as the next structured restoration pathway milestone.

latestArticle.title: Claude Fable 5 Resurfaces in Android App as NSA Breach Testimony Reshapes Ban

ipoTracker.status: Confirmed SEC filing June 8; GPT-5.6 launch window opened today (June 22) — no official announcement; 42% odds of June 22–28 release; Noam Shazeer joined as Lead for Architecture Research; CFO may push to 2027
ipoTracker.process: OpenAI confirmed its confidential S-1 filing with the SEC on June 8, 2026. Goldman Sachs, Morgan Stanley, and JPMorgan are leading. CEO Sam Altman expects a listing within the next year; CFO Sarah Friar has reportedly expressed concern about operational readiness, with a potential delay to 2027. GPT-5.6's prediction market window opened today (June 22); Polymarket assigns 42% probability to a formal June 22–28 release and 58% to no release by June 28. Noam Shazeer — co-inventor of the Transformer — joined OpenAI as Lead for Architecture Research on June 18. Sensor Tower found ChatGPT's global AI assistant market share fell to 46.4% by late May — the first time below 50%. EU AI Act chatbot enforcement begins August 2, 2026, adding compliance milestones to the pre-IPO period.
ipoTracker.note: Noam Shazeer joining (June 18) is the most high-profile researcher hire in years. GPT-5.6 launch window is open as of today. Sub-50% ChatGPT market share and EU AI Act enforcement (August 2) are key S-1 risk factors to watch.

latestArticle.title: OpenAI's CFO reportedly wants to delay the IPO from 2026 to 2027

ipoTracker.status: SPCX trading ~$210–220; up ~56% from $135 IPO price; $60B Cursor acquisition pending Q3 close; Grok for Word free add-in live
ipoTracker.process: SpaceX priced at $135/share on June 11, raising $75 billion at a $1.75 trillion valuation in the largest IPO ever recorded. Shares peaked at $225.64 on June 16, trading around $210–220 — up roughly 56% from the IPO price. SpaceX confirmed via SEC Form 8-K the $60B all-stock acquisition of Anysphere (Cursor AI); expected to close Q3 2026 pending regulatory and shareholder approval. xAI was folded into SpaceX as the SpaceXAI division in February 2026; all Grok/AI exposure trades via ticker SPCX. Grok for Microsoft Word (free add-in, launched June 18) and Grok 4.3 GA on Amazon Bedrock are the latest product milestones.
ipoTracker.confidence: Confirmed; public company as of June 12, 2026
ipoTracker.note: Cursor acquisition (Q3 2026 close) and Grok for Word (free) are the key near-term milestones. Cursor had $2B+ ARR and half of Fortune 500 as clients — the AI coding acquisition that most directly challenges Anthropic's Claude Code and GitHub Copilot.

latestArticle.title: SpaceX acquires Cursor for $60 billion

ipoTracker.status: IPO completed May 2026; Q1 2026 earnings release tomorrow (June 23) — first public earnings; analysts estimate $56.65M revenue, loss of $0.14/share
ipoTracker.process: Cerebras completed its Nasdaq IPO in May 2026 under ticker CBRS, opening 89% above its IPO price on May 14, and trades around $218. Q1 2026 financial results are scheduled for release after market close tomorrow, June 23 — Cerebras' first quarterly report as a public company. Analysts estimate revenue of approximately $56.65M and a loss of $0.14/share. Key investor focus: how fast the $24.6 billion backlog is converting to revenue, and whether the company has found a major customer outside Abu Dhabi. The earnings call is at 2 pm PT / 5 pm ET.
ipoTracker.note: The June 23 Q1 2026 earnings release is tomorrow — the next major AI infrastructure public-company earnings catalyst after today's CoreWeave Nasdaq-100 inclusion.

latestArticle.title: Cerebras Systems Sets Date of First-Quarter 2026 Financial Results

ipoTracker.status: CRWV joined Nasdaq-100 today (June 22); Russell 3000 June 27; backlog ~$131B; $2.2B Chicago data-center lease added
ipoTracker.process: CoreWeave completed its IPO in March 2025 (priced at $40/share) and trades on Nasdaq as CRWV. The stock joined the Nasdaq-100 Index before market open today, June 22, just 15 months post-IPO. Russell 3000 inclusion follows June 27. The company completed a $3.55B high-yield bond deal. Q1 2026 revenue was $2.1 billion (+112% YoY). Contracted backlog is approaching approximately $131 billion. A new $2.2 billion Chicago-area data-center lease adds contracted revenue visibility. 2026 capex floor raised to $31 billion for Vera Rubin NVL72 buildout serving Meta, Anthropic, and OpenAI.
ipoTracker.note: Nasdaq-100 inclusion happened today (June 22). The next catalyst is Cerebras Q1 earnings tomorrow (June 23) as the AI infrastructure earnings comparable.

Anthropic expressed interest in developing multiple gigawatts of orbital AI compute capacity. Public financial terms remain undisclosed. The Fable 5/Mythos 5 export suspension affects model distribution but not the underlying compute contract.

Project Rainier already runs more than one million Trainium2 chips for Claude workloads. AgentCore GA at AWS Summit NYC (June 17–20) incorporates Managed Knowledge Base, Web Search, Agent Optimization, and Guardrails as the enterprise deployment layer for Claude on Bedrock. Amazon confirmed Claude models (outside Fable 5 and Mythos 5) remain available to AWS customers.
partnershipTracker.note: AgentCore GA at AWS Summit NYC is the most significant recent operational development. No new contract terms. The Fable/Mythos suspension does not affect other Claude models on Bedrock.

Broadcom reconfirmed Anthropic as one of its six core custom-silicon customers; FY2026 AI target is $56 billion and FY2027 exceeds $100 billion. The Fable/Mythos suspension does not affect this infrastructure agreement. Broadcom's ex-dividend date passed today (June 22), with $0.65/share payable June 30.

partnershipTracker.terms: NVIDIA and Mistral AI launched Mistral Compute — a sovereign AI GPU cloud platform for European customers and governments — at VivaTech 2026 (closing June 20). Mistral's Bruyères-le-Châtel data center near Paris is expected to open by end of June 2026 with 13,800 NVIDIA GB300 systems, forming the first node in a planned 200 MW European compute roadmap. NVIDIA reaffirmed Mistral as the flagship of France's sovereign AI strategy.
partnershipTracker.confidence: Confirmed by both companies at VivaTech
partnershipTracker.note: Mistral Compute launched June 20. Paris data center opening is expected by end of June — within 8 days. Mistral's Vibe agent and Emmi AI acquisition (industrial AI simulation) are the most recent product developments.

latestArticle.title: France Advances Europe's AI Future With NVIDIA Technologies
